commit_scope: Repair native HTTP mutation persistence for repeated production traffic by deriving graph_node_revisions ids from route prefix, node id, and revision number.

local_validation.summary: The public deploy canary for PR #2670 reached native-kernel but returned 503 at persistence stage pg_exec. The response proved the kernel route and mounted config carrier were active; the SQL used fixed revision ids such as rev-native-public-gate-create-idea, which can collide under repeated production creates. The Form SQL builder now emits revision ids as route-prefix:node-id:revision-number, and the public-gate harness includes a no-reset repeated-create collision probe for no-header default and X-Form-Native-Public-Gate routes.

known_limitations.observed: The repair has not yet been deployed at commit creation time. Post-merge verification must rerun scripts/verify_kernel_canary_public_gate.sh https://api.coherencycoin.com and the Hostinger Auto Deploy workflow.

This repair keeps ordinary public no-header mutation traffic outside the kernel-router default route. It only repairs native persistence for traffic that already reaches the kernel canary/default route.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR creation, and GitHub checks for codex/native-http-revision-id-fix-20260609.

deploy_validation.notes: Run after merge/deploy. The expected treatment result is 202 with X-Form-Router:native-kernel, executes:true, db_execution=performed-by-http-native-persistence, and persistence.executes=true.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Repeated native HTTP create requests no longer collide on graph_node_revisions.id. Local proof now runs two default creates and two public-gate creates without schema reset and verifies two node rows plus two distinct revision ids for each route family.
test_flows: form-kernel-rust route check -> every name resolves

phase_gate.reason: The repeated-create production collision is fixed locally. The deployed header-gated canary must pass before any public no-header mutation flip.
